title: ayenia oblongifolia
intro: Ayenia oblongifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the wild tobacco or Indian tobacco, due to its leaves resembling those of Nicotiana rustica. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ayenia oblongifolia include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and coughs. Herbal preparations of Ayenia oblongifolia can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
